classes.name: SABA (Rescue)
classes.description: Short-acting beta-agonist for acute bronchospasm; quick relief, not a controller.
classes.plainDescription: The inhaler you grab when you suddenly can't breathe. Works in minutes.

classes.name: ICS (Maintenance)
classes.description: Inhaled corticosteroid; the anti-inflammatory foundation of daily asthma control.
classes.plainDescription: A daily inhaler that calms airway inflammation so attacks happen less.

classes.name: ICS/LABA (Combo)
classes.description: Steroid + long-acting bronchodilator in one inhaler for moderate-to-severe asthma or COPD.
classes.plainDescription: A daily inhaler that combines the steroid with a long-acting opener.

classes.name: LAMA
classes.description: Long-acting muscarinic antagonist. FDA-indicated for COPD maintenance, not asthma monotherapy; not for rescue.
classes.plainDescription: A once-daily opener used mainly for COPD — not a rescue inhaler.

classes.name: Biologics & Non-Inhalers
classes.description: Specialty-pharmacy biologics (anti-IgE, anti-IL5, anti-IL4) for severe asthma. Separate PA pathway; not inhalers.
classes.plainDescription: Injection or infusion drugs for severe asthma — handled differently from inhalers.

records.verificationNote: Fetched the PDF directly via curl with a browser User-Agent (fm.formularynavigator.com/FBO/4/Virginia_PDL_English.pdf, 200 OK, confirmed real 153-page PDF) and extracted full text with PyPDF2. Page 1 caption confirms 'VA Cardinal Care Formulary, Medicaid-Approved Preferred Drug List, Effective May 1, 2026'. Page 17-18, under '*ANTIASTHMATIC AND BRONCHODILATOR AGENTS*' > '*ADRENERGIC COMBINATIONS***', lists (notes read verbatim): ADVAIR DISKUS (no note), ADVAIR HFA (no note), ANORO ELLIPTA (AL - excluded from this record, it is a LAMA/LABA combo not ICS/LABA), COMBIVENT RESPIMAT (no note - excluded, it is a SAMA/SABA combo), DULERA 100-5/200-5 mcg (no note), DULERA 50-5 mcg (AL), ipratropium-albuterol inhalation solution (no note - excluded, SAMA/SABA), STIOLTO RESPIMAT (AL - excluded, LAMA/LABA combo), SYMBICORT (no note), TRELEGY ELLIPTA (no note - triple ICS/LAMA/LABA therapy, included as an alternative since it contains an ICS+LABA backbone plus a LAMA). Class composition was cross-checked against known pharmacology (Advair=fluticasone/salmeterol, Dulera=mometasone/formoterol, Symbicort=budesonide/formoterol, Trelegy=fluticasone/umeclidinium/vilanterol) to correctly separate true ICS/LABA combinations from the LAMA/LABA (Anoro, Stiolto) and SAMA/SABA (Combivent, generic ipratropium-albuterol) combinations also listed under the same 'Adrenergic Combinations' heading - this classification is inferred from established drug identity, not a label printed in the PDL itself. No generic version of any ICS/LABA combination inhaler appears anywhere in this subsection or in a full-document search for 'fluticasone-salmeterol', 'budesonide-formoterol', or 'mometasone-formoterol' as generic entries - all are brand-only, hence boglActive is true by necessity (no generic exists on this PDL to prefer over brand). Symbicort was selected as the single preferredAgent since it has the broadest name recognition among the clean (no-note) options and no restriction; the other clean brands are equally covered alternatives. No class-wide step-therapy criterion appears near this section.

The section lists exactly five agents: albuterol sulfate inhalation nebulization solution, albuterol sulfate oral syrup, albuterol sulfate oral tablet, SEREVENT DISKUS (salmeterol, brand, with 'AL; QL' notes), terbutaline sulfate oral tablet, and VENTOLIN HFA INHALATION AEROSOL SOLUTION (brand albuterol, with 'PA' note). Notably, generic albuterol sulfate HFA inhaler (the standard rescue inhaler form) does not appear as its own line item in this PDL section -- only the brand VENTOLIN HFA is listed, carrying PA. Per this project's Anthem HealthKeepers Plus Cardinal Care PDL-following convention (confirmed for the ACE-inhibitor and diabetes classes already in formulary.json), and per the PDL legend (page 1: 'Brand name drug: Uppercase in bold type; Generic drug: Lowercase in plain type'), a generic albuterol sulfate HFA inhaler is the de facto covered/preferred rescue inhaler in Virginia Medicaid formularies generally, but this specific PDL page does not print a bare 'albuterol sulfate inhalation aerosol HFA' line -- so the exact preferred-agent restriction status (PA yes/no on generic HFA specifically) is inferred from the absence of a PA note on the other albuterol dosage forms listed (nebulization solution, oral syrup, oral tablet all carry no restriction codes), not read verbatim for the HFA inhaler form itself. Strength/sig for albuterol HFA (90 mcg/actuation, 2 puffs q4-6h PRN) is standard clinical dosing convention, not stated in this PDL (which lists drug/tier/PA/QL/AL notes only, no dosing directions). No class-wide step-therapy criterion or BOGL note appears in or near this section.
